source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two long metal rods of length \( 0.50\text{m} \) lie parallel on a frictionless table, connected by springs of unstretched length \( l_0 \) and force constant \( k \). When current \( I \) flows, the springs stretch by \( x = 0.40\text{cm} \) at \( I = 8.05\text{A} \), and by \( x = 0.80\text{cm} \) at \( I = 13.1\text{A} \). What current is required for each spring to stretch \( 1.00\,\text{cm} \)?

multi_step: The question gives two separate current-stretch observations: x=0.40 cm at I=8.05 A and x=0.80 cm at I=13.1 A
Solving for the target stretch requires combining multiple conditions rather than reading off a single value
This is a physics setup involving rods, springs, and current, which typically requires forming and manipulating a relation before computing the final current
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and answer_text is a specific numeric result: 16.0A
There are 4 multiple-choice options, including 16.0A, enabling straightforward answer checking
registry_passed=true, with question and answer both present in the registry
